1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 850 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Tri Durability — Anatomical Adaptation B (Hinge/Vertical Press/Pull)
1.description: Base Week 1 (AA phase) — Full-body tissue work. Light loads, high reps (20-25), slow tempos. Loads should feel easy; the goal is connective-tissue resilience, not muscle fatigue. RIR 3-4 throughout. Equipment — Required: Bands.

strength_exercises.name: Calf Raises (Bilateral)
strength_exercises.notes: Achilles + plantar fascia resilience for run volume

1.name: Run Intervals — 4×1000m
1.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1000 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Long Ride — 4.5 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 14 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

4.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
4.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

4.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1300 yd
4.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery); 4×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

4.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
4.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

4.name: Recovery Swim — 1100 yd
4.description: Warm up 200 yd easy. 7×100 yd at easy aerobic effort (20 sec rest). Cool down 200 yd.

7.name: Tri Durability — Maximum Strength B
7.description: Late Base Week 7 (MS phase) — Heavy compounds at 6-8 reps, RIR 2. Same movement patterns you've been training in AA, now at race-meaningful loads. Squat / hinge / push / pull — 3 sets each, full-body. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells.

7.name: Run Intervals — 7×1000m
7.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 7×1000m at 10K / tempo pace with 90 sec jog recovery between. Cool down 10 min. Focus on consistent splits, not all-out.

Cool down 200 yd.

7.name: Long Run — 14 mi
7.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

8.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
8.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

8.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1300 yd
8.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery); 4×50 Fist Swim (early vertical forearm). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

8.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
8.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

8.name: Recovery Swim — 1200 yd
8.description: Warm up 200 yd easy. 8×100 yd at easy aerobic effort (20 sec rest). Cool down 200 yd.

8.name: Easy Run — 2 mi
8.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

8.name: Long Ride — 0.8 hr
8.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

8.name: Long Run — 3 mi
8.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

9.name: Bike Threshold — 2×20 min
9.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 2×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

9.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1000 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (11 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Endurance Swim — 1000 yd
9.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). Main set — 2×150 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B
9.description: Build Week 9 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort.  Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

9.name: VO2max Run — 4×3 min
9.description: Warm up 10 min easy. 4×3 min at Z5 (hard — controlled sprint, not all-out) with 90 sec float recovery. Cool down 10 min. Builds raw aerobic ceiling.

9.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1200 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd. 7×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd. If you don't have a 100yd pace baseline yet, swim at an effort where you can hold a short conversation but feel like you're working. Aim for the same effort on every repeat — consistency matters more than hitting a specific number.

9.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
9.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

9.name: Brick — Bike 3.8 hr
9.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

9.name: Brick — Run 5 mi off the bike
9.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

9.name: Long Run — 14 mi
9.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

14.name: Bike VO2max — 3×5 min
14.description: Warm up 15 min. 3×5 min at 110–120% FTP (Zone 5) with 3 min easy recovery. Cool down 10 min. Short, maximal efforts — go hard.

14.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 1000 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (10 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Endurance Swim — 1100 yd
14.description: Warm up 400 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation). Main set — 2×200 yd easy aerobic with 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Tri Durability — Strength Maintenance B (Reduced)
14.description: Race-Specific Week 14 (SM phase) — Maintain strength built in MS with 2 sets at 8-10 reps and moderate load (~70% 1RM). Movement quality, not maximal effort. Race-specific volume reduced — minimize fatigue ahead of the race. Equipment — Required: Dumbbells, Pull-up Bar, Bands.

14.name: Marathon Pace Run — 3 mi
14.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 3 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

14.name: Swim Threshold — 1200 yd
14.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

14.name: Easy Run + Strides — 3 mi
14.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. After the cool-down, 4 × 20 sec strides at ~5K pace effort with 30 sec walk recovery. Strides wake up fast-twitch fibers and improve running economy — not speedwork; relaxed and fast.

14.name: Brick — Bike 4.5 hr
14.description: Race-simulation brick — ride 225 min at Zone 2 to build durability, then close with 45 min at expected race power (Z3, ~0.78-0.82 IF for 70.3 / 0.62-0.68 IF for full IM). Stay aero through the close. Transition quickly into the run.

14.name: Brick — Run 6.5 mi off the bike
14.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Target race pace last half.

14.name: Marathon Pace Run — 4 mi
14.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 4 mi at Ironman / marathon run pace (Z3), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

15.name: Bike VO2max — 4×5 min

Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
16.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

16.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 1400 yd
16.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Sighting Drill (head-up race-day navigation); 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 5×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

16.name: Recovery Swim — 1200 yd
16.description: Warm up 200 yd easy. 8×100 yd at easy aerobic effort (20 sec rest). Cool down 200 yd.

16.name: Easy Run — 2 mi
16.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

16.name: Long Ride — 0.8 hr
16.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

16.name: Long Run — 2.5 mi
16.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

18.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
18.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

18.name: Swim Threshold — 1200 yd

18.name: Long Run — 14 mi

19.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
19.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

19.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
19.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

19.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 700 yd
19.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

19.name: Tri Durability — Taper Light Session
19.description: Race week — one light session early in the week (Monday or Tuesday). 30 minutes, bodyweight or 40% 1RM, mobility focus. Skip-optional if you feel fresh and want the day for recovery. After this session, no strength until the post-race recovery week. Equipment — Required: Bands.

19.name: Easy Run + Strides — 4 mi
19.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue. 20.name: Long Run — 2.3 mi
20.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

21.name: Bike Openers — 30 min
21.description: Short pre-race sharpener. 20 min easy Z2, then 3×30-second fast-pedal bursts. Legs should feel snappy afterwards.

21.name: Moderate Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
21.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at moderate effort — sustainable and conversational (15 sec rest). Focus on consistent splits. Hands-only by default; paddles optional for occasional repeats only (not the full set)—protects shoulders on high-volume aerobic blocks. Cool down 200 yd.

21.name: Technique Aerobic Swim — 700 yd

21.name: Tri Durability — Taper Light Session
21.description: Race week — one light session early in the week (Monday or Tuesday). 30 minutes, bodyweight or 40% 1RM, mobility focus. Skip-optional if you feel fresh and want the day for recovery. After this session, no strength until the post-race recovery week. Equipment — Required: Bands.

21.name: Easy Run — 4 mi
21.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

21.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
21.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

21.name: Test full race
21.description: Race day. Swim 2.4mi → Bike 112mi → Run 26.2mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

9: Week 9 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (3.75h vs 4.5h). Lengthening toward 4.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
13: Week 13 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (3.75h vs 4.5h). Lengthening toward 4.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
14: Week 14 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 13.5+ mi long run for durability.

17: Week 17 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 13.5+ mi long run for durability.
